headline_draft: Claude Code adds security stability update with tighter permissions and progress tracking for long-running tasks
summary: Anthropic shipped a broad stability and safety update to Claude Code (July 19, 2026) featuring tighter permission checks, safer Bash and PowerShell handling, improved background session cleanup, and progress heartbeats for multi-step workflows. The update also adds an EndConversation tool and live MCP connector data for published artifacts, plus screen reader mode and expanded collaboration options. Claude Code now restricts auto-running of verification and code-review skills to explicit invocations only, reducing unnecessary overhead and improving trust in autonomous workflows.
why_pm: For PMs building with AI agents or prototyping, tighter safety controls and explicit permission models reduce risk in production workflows. Progress heartbeats and better session management make long-running research or automation tasks more reliable, lowering the friction of delegating multi-step PM work to Claude Code.
key_facts: Tighter permission checks and safer shell handling
Improved background session cleanup and telemetry
Added EndConversation tool for session control
quotes: Claude Code changes /verify and /code-review to run only when invoked directly — no auto-running
